hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in TM5/ECL2, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific ECL3 pocket, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient selectivity. The focus must shift to explicitly breaking conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin and achieve true selectivity over native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 that may be driving off-target binding while preserving pharmacophore positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.
These mutations are designed to break conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving overall binding to SSTR2.
